forecast-period.text: A series of low pressure systems passing through the Bight will extend cold fronts that will cross over SA through to the end of the week. A high pressure system is then forecast to move into the Bight from late Friday and extend a ridge across the state over the weekend.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Tuesday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 30 knots in the west.
1 to 1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres in the west.
Southwesterly 2.5 metres.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the west.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Gale Warning for Wednesday for Central Coast
forecast-period.text: North to northwesterly 20 to 30 knots tending west to northwesterly 25 to 30 knots in the late morning and early afternoon. Winds reaching up to 35 knots in the late evening.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres in the west by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
West to northwesterly 30 to 35 knots becoming westerly 25 to 30 knots before dawn then increasing to 25 to 35 knots in the middle of the day.
3 metres.
Westerly 4 to 6 metres, tending southwesterly 6 to 7 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the evening.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 7 metres, decreasing to 4 to 5 metres during the evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
Northerly 15 to 20 knots.
1 to 1.5 metres.
West to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Wednesday for Investigator Strait
forecast-period.text: Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning northwesterly 25 to 30 knots in the morning.
1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
West to southwesterly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing to around 1 metre during the morning, then tending westerly 1 to 2 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.
Northwesterly 30 to 35 knots tending westerly 25 to 30 knots before dawn then increasing to 25 to 35 knots in the middle of the day.
2 to 3 metres.
Westerly 2 to 3 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
West to southwesterly 20 to 30 knots tending south to southwesterly below 10 knots during the day.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 metre during the afternoon or evening.
West to southwesterly 2 to 4 metres, decreasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Tuesday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 30 knots west of Kangaroo Island.
1.5 metres, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres west of Kangaroo Island.
West to southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Partly cloudy. The chance of a thunderstorm.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Wednesday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 20 to 30 knots turning northwesterly 25 to 30 knots during the morning.
1.5 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 2 to 3 metres.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.
Westerly 30 to 35 knots.
3 to 4 metres.
Westerly 3 to 5 metres, increasing to 4 to 7 metres around midday, then tending southwesterly 6 to 8 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers, with possible small hail in the late afternoon and evening. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning.
Southwesterly 25 to 35 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots during the afternoon.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 2 metres during the afternoon.
Southwesterly 6 to 8 metres, decreasing to 5 to 6 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.text: Northerly 15 to 20 knots.
Around 1 metre, increasing to 1 to 1.5 metres offshore.
Southwesterly 2 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Wednesday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Northerly 20 to 30 knots tending northwesterly 25 to 30 knots in the early afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1.5 metres by early evening, then increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm in the morning and afternoon.
Northwesterly 25 to 35 knots turning westerly 30 to 35 knots during the morning.
2.5 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres, tending westerly 2 to 4 metres during the morning, then increasing to 4 to 6 metres by early evening.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm south of Salt Creek in the morning and afternoon.
Westerly 30 to 35 knots turning southwesterly 20 to 30 knots during the morning then decreasing to 15 to 20 knots during the day.
2 to 3 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Southwesterly 5 to 8 metres, decreasing to 5 to 6 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
